summary: Iteration 2 testing: workspaces + members + scoping + permissions, share links (note + PDF + invalid slug + public_owner_id absent), and deck chat (Claude Sonnet 4.5). 17/17 new backend tests pass. Re-ran iteration-1 backend_test.py: 29/29 pass after a tiny test-side relaxation (cross-user folder delete now correctly returns 403 via workspace-membership check; updated assertion to accept 403 or 404). Frontend Playwright: created/switched/deleted team workspace, opened settings, sent pending invite (visible in member list), shared PDF via Share dialog, opened /s/:slug page (loaded), invalid slug fallback shown, disabled share, opened Chat tab on FileViewer, sent ESG question, got accurate assistant answer naming Environmental/Social/Governance, persisted across reload, cleared via Clear button.

issues: Trigger renders 'WORKSPACE' label and name concatenated as one accessible name ('UWorkspaceUI_TEST_WS_...'), making screen-reader output confusing. Cosmetic only.

action_items: (Optional) Public PDF page: in playwright the public-pdf-iframe and download button were not visible immediately after navigation; the page returned 200 but content needed time to render. Recommend a short loading state with data-testid on the public page wrapper and rendering iframe immediately after fetch resolves.
(Optional) Split server.py (1100+ lines) into routers (workspaces/items/share/chat) as previously flagged in iteration_1.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew to 1102 lines; recommend per-domain routers (workspaces.py, share.py, chat.py).
CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True remains (carry-over from iteration_1) — unsafe and rejected by some browsers.
Chat endpoint stores full extracted text in system prompt for every turn; for large decks (40k cap) this incurs Claude tokens on every call. Consider summarising or chunk-retrieving for cost.
Public download (/public/items/{slug}/download) loads full object into memory (StreamingResponse(io.BytesIO(data))). Consider true streaming from storage for big files.
Invite endpoint allows owner role only as bootstrap; viewer role hierarchy is enforced via ROLE_RANK and looks correct.
delete_workspace cascade not visible in this review — verify items/folders in deleted workspace are either purged or reassigned (out of test scope here).

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested critical flows (workspaces, share, chat)

seed_data_creation: Test runs create TEST_-prefixed workspaces, folders, notes, PDFs, and chat docs. All are deleted inline in fixtures/finally blocks. A few orphan member rows for deleted workspaces may remain (not user-visible).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two backend test files live under /app/backend/tests/: backend_test.py (iter1, 29 tests) and test_iteration2.py (iter2, 17 tests). Run: pytest backend/tests/ -v. The chat tests use reportlab to build a tiny PDF with 'three pillars of ESG' content; Claude reliably answers in ~5–8s. ShareDialog opens via 'file-share-button' / 'note-share-button'; deck chat panel opens via 'file-chat-button' (collapsed by default). All key data-testids verified: workspace-switcher-trigger, workspace-switcher-content, workspace-new-button, workspace-create-dialog/input/confirm, workspace-settings-link, workspace-settings-page, invite-form/email-input/role-select/submit-button, member-row-*, workspace-delete-button/confirm, share-dialog/enable-button/url-input/copy-button/disable-button, public-item-page/public-pdf-iframe/public-download-button/public-open-link/public-note-content, panel-tab-summary/panel-tab-chat, chat-input/send/clear-button/messages, chat-message-user/assistant/chat-sending.
